title: Domain types, thresholds, and piece values
description: Plan 1 Task 3. Create src/domain/types.ts (canonical WeaknessTag/MoveClass/MoveAnalysis/WeaknessProfile), thresholds.ts (CP_LOSS_THRESHOLDS), pieceValues.ts. Source of truth for the taxonomy. See plan Task 3.

title: Repo conventions, guardrails, and git hooks
description: Plan 1 Task 2. CLAUDE.md already exists (created during beads setup) — extend, do not overwrite, preserving the beads integration block. Create the 11 .claude/rules/ files, .githooks/{pre-commit,commit-msg}, .gitleaks.toml, .env.example; wire core.hooksPath. See plan Task 2.

title: Scaffold Next.js project and tooling
description: Plan 1 Task 1. Create package.json, tsconfig (strict), next.config.ts, eslint.config.mjs, .prettierrc.json, vitest.config.ts, and the minimal App Router shell. pnpm install; verify typecheck passes. See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-chess-coach-foundation.md (Task 1).

title: Plan 1: Foundation & Domain Core
description: Scaffold the chess-coach Next.js project, repo conventions/guardrails + git hooks, and the engine-independent chess analysis core. Detailed plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-chess-coach-foundation.md

title: detectWeakOpening tagger
description: Plan 1 Task 7. TDD: flag a premature queen sortie within the early opening window. See plan Task 7.

title: aggregateProfile (MoveAnalysis[] -> WeaknessProfile)
description: Plan 1 Task 8. TDD: aggregate per-move analyses into tag frequencies, average cpLoss, class counts, and up to 3 examples per tag; handle the empty case. Final step runs full suite + typecheck + lint. See plan Task 8.

title: detectPhase (fen -> GamePhase)
description: Plan 1 Task 5. TDD: opening (<=move 10), endgame (<=6 non-pawn/non-king pieces), else middlegame. See plan Task 5.

title: classifyMove (cpLoss -> MoveClass)
description: Plan 1 Task 4. TDD: classify a move by centipawn loss into best/good/inaccuracy/mistake/blunder, clamping negatives. See plan Task 4.

title: detectHangingPiece tagger
description: Plan 1 Task 6. TDD: in the position after the user's move, can the opponent win material via capture (higher-value, or free minor+ with no recapture). See plan Task 6.
